title: bencomia spinosa
intro: Bencomia spinosa, also known as the San Cristobal Island tree daisy or yerba buena, is a shrub native to the Galapagos Islands. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and even help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Bencomia spinosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional medicine practices as a topical treatment for skin conditions and wounds.
